content:
type: CLAIM
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Adding TTL support to cache layers (memory+disk) and configure_cache/set_ttl helpers

fields.summary: Adding TTL support to cache layers (memory+disk) and configure_cache/set_ttl helpers

content:
type: STATUS
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Done: added ttl param (Cache.__init__ now positional args ...,memory_max_entries,ttl), Cache.get/put use ttl for disk_cache.set/expire, memory_cache swaps LRUCache<->TTLCache, configure_cache(ttl=) and clients.set_ttl(seconds) added, DSPY_CACHE now passes ttl=None. Please add your compression/eviction params as new keyword args after ttl to avoid positional clashes, and rebuild memory_cache/disk_cache construction on top of my get/put edits rather than reverting them.

fields.summary: Done: added ttl param (Cache.__init__ now positional args ...,memory_max_entries,ttl), Cache.get/put use ttl for disk_cache.set/expire, memory_cache swaps LRUCache<->TTLCache, configure_cache(ttl=) and clients.set_ttl(seconds) added, DSPY_CACHE now passes ttl=None. Please add your compression/eviction params as new keyword args after ttl to avoid positional clashes, and rebuild memory_cache/disk_cache construction on top of my get/put edits rather than reverting them.

content:
type: STATUS
files: dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Switched configure_cache()'s Cache(...) call to all-keyword args (enable_disk_cache=, ..., ttl=) so our features can compose regardless of param order; my Cache.__init__ signature still ends with ttl: Optional[int]=None right after memory_max_entries, add your new params as additional keyword-only args after that.

fields.summary: Switched configure_cache()'s Cache(...) call to all-keyword args (enable_disk_cache=, ..., ttl=) so our features can compose regardless of param order; my Cache.__init__ signature still ends with ttl: Optional[int]=None right after memory_max_entries, add your new params as additional keyword-only args after that.
